growth_records.medium: Mineral medium (ammonium mineral salts with phosphate-carbonate buffer)

up.method: reported specific ammonia oxidation rate qNH3 (days 9-11, 3-day sampling)

sec.method: calculated = qNH3 x stoichiometry; NH3->NO2- was stoichiometric (N balance 61.0 mmol/L ~= 60.1 mmol/L NO2-), so qNO2- ~= qNH3 = 24.73

yields.Yxs: 0.40 g DCW per mol NH3 (3-day, days 9-11); 0.42 g DCW/mol NH3 for 10-day period (days 7-16)

growth_records.cite: Sedlacek et al. Transcriptomic Response of Nitrosomonas europaea Transitioned from Ammonia- to Oxygen-Limited Steady-State Growth. mSystems (2020). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00562-19. [PMC6967387]
growth_records.snippet: On days 9, 10, and 11...N. europaea consumed NH3 at a rate (qNH3) of 24.73 +/- 0.53 mmol g (dry cell weight)-1 h-1 with an apparent growth yield (Y) of 0.40 +/- 0.01 g (dry cell weight) mol-1 NH3. dilution rate/specific growth rate (mu) of
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state so mu = D = 0.01 /h (reported directly; doubling time ~70 h, consistent with ln2/0.01 = 69.3 h). qNH3 reported directly = 24.73 mmol/gDCW/h (3-day, days 9-11) or 24.04 mmol/gDCW/h (10-day period, Table 1). O2 uptake not quantified. Nitrite secretion computed from stoichiometric NH3->NO2- conversion (N balance = 61.0 +/-1.7 mmol/L vs NO2- 60.1 +/-1.4 mmol/L, ~98% recovery), giving qNO2- ~= qNH3 = 24.73 mmol/gDCW/h. Yxs reported = 0.40 (3-day)/0.42 (10-day) g DCW per mol NH3. Feed = 60 mmol/L NH4+ x 0.24 L/day = 14.4 mmol NH4+/day.

growth_records.medium: Mineral medium (ammonium mineral salts with phosphate-carbonate buffer)

up.method: reported specific ammonia oxidation rate qNH3 (days 28-30, 3-day sampling)

sec.method: calculated: only 77.5% of oxidized NH4+ recovered as NO2-, so qNO2- = 0.775 x qNH3 = 0.775 x 28.51 = 22.1
calculated by N-balance difference: 22.5% of qNH3 lost as N gas = 0.225 x 28.51 = 6.4 mmol N/gDCW/h; individual gas fluxes not resolved in this study

yields.Yxs: 0.35 g DCW per mol NH3 (3-day, days 28-30); 0.38 g DCW/mol NH3 for 10-day period (days 23-32)

growth_records.cite: Sedlacek et al. Transcriptomic Response of Nitrosomonas europaea Transitioned from Ammonia- to Oxygen-Limited Steady-State Growth. mSystems (2020). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00562-19. [PMC6967387]
growth_records.snippet: During days sampled for O2-limited growth transcriptomes (days 28, 29, and 30), the qNH3 was significantly higher (28.51 +/- 1.13 mmol g [dry cell weight]-1 h-1; P <= 0.05), while Y was significantly lower (0.35 +/- 0.01 g [dry cell weight]
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state, mu = D = 0.01 /h kept constant across both conditions (stated: 'specific growth rate...kept constant during both NH3- and O2-limited growth'); td ~70 h. qNH3 reported = 28.51 mmol/gDCW/h (3-day, days 28-30) or 26.44 (10-day, Table 1). NH4+ consumption non-stoichiometric under O2 limitation: 51.8% of supplied NH4+ consumed (31.1 mmol/L), of which only 77.5% appeared as NO2- (24.1 mmol/L). qNO2- computed = 0.775 x 28.51 = 22.1 mmol/gDCW/h. Remaining 22.5% lost as NH2OH/NO/N2O -> 0.225 x 28.51 = 6.4 mmol N/gDCW/h (aggregate; individual NO/N2O fluxes not measured here). Yxs reported 0.35 (3-day)/0.38 (10-day). O2 uptake rate not quantified.

growth_records.medium: Mineral salts nitrifier medium (chemostat feed, as previously described, ref 56)
growth_records.cite: Mellbye et al. Genome-Scale, Constraint-Based Modeling of Nitrogen Oxide Fluxes during Coculture of Nitrosomonas europaea and Nitrobacter winogradskyi. mSystems (2018). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00170-17. [PMC5864417]
growth_records.snippet: Nitrosomonas europaea (ATCC 19718)... were routinely cultivated at 30C in batch and chemostat culture as previously described. Chemostat cultures were maintained in steady state at a dilution rate of 0.015 h-1.
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady-state: mu = D = 0.015 h-1 (verbatim dilution rate). Doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.015 = 46.2 h. Temperature 30C stated. Chemostat feed medium composition not given in this paper (cited to ref 56) -> composition [] / stated false. pH of chemostat not stated (batch experimental medium was pH 7.8, but that is a separate condition).

growth_records.strain: N. europaea ATCC 19718 + N. winogradskyi Nb-255
growth_records.medium: Mineral salts nitrifier medium (chemostat feed, as previously described, ref 56)
growth_records.cite: Mellbye et al. Genome-Scale, Constraint-Based Modeling of Nitrogen Oxide Fluxes during Coculture of Nitrosomonas europaea and Nitrobacter winogradskyi. mSystems (2018). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00170-17. [PMC5864417]
growth_records.snippet: a steady-state coculture chemostat of N. europaea and N. winogradskyi... Chemostat cultures were maintained in steady state at a dilution rate of 0.015 h-1.
growth_records.notes: Coculture chemostat at steady state: community mu = D = 0.015 h-1 (verbatim). td = ln2/0.015 = 46.2 h. Two-organism coculture; culture_mode set to chemostat (continuous) with co-culture noted in culture_detail. Chemostat feed medium cited to ref 56 -> composition [] / stated false.

growth_records.medium: Experimental batch mineral salts medium (N. europaea, ammonium)

up.method: initial 5 mM NH4+ (from 2.5 mM (NH4)2SO4) fully oxidized over the 10 h experiment; time course in Fig 1A, no numeric specific rate stated

sec.method: measured; 83.5 +/- 3.1 uM after 1 h then consumed by 4 h (concentration, not specific rate)
measured headspace peak 30.2 +/- 5.7 ppm during active NH3 oxidation
measured headspace peak 42.1 +/- 0.9 ppm
model simulation peak ~1 umol h-1 per 5 ml culture = 1 umol/h / 0.005 L = 0.2 mmol/L/h (dFBA-predicted maximum, not measured net rate)

growth_records.cite: Mellbye et al. Genome-Scale, Constraint-Based Modeling of Nitrogen Oxide Fluxes during Coculture of Nitrosomonas europaea and Nitrobacter winogradskyi. mSystems (2018). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00170-17. [PMC5864417]
growth_records.snippet: Experimental batch mineral salts medium for N. europaea contained 2.5 mM (NH4)2SO4, 0.75 mM MgSO4, 0.1 mM CaCl2, 12.5 mM KH2PO4, 1.25 mM NaH2PO4.H2O, 2.3 mM Na2CO3, and trace elements... pH... adjusted to 7.8.
growth_records.notes: Batch medium composition fully stated (verbatim, above). Conditions: 30C, pH 7.8, 200 rpm, closed batch 10 h, OD600 0.2 inoculum, aerobic (sufficient O2 in headspace). No specific growth rate reported for the batch (OD600 measured but no slope/mu given) -> growth_rate null. Uptake substrate NH4+: initial 5 mM NH4+ (2.5 mM (NH4)2SO4 x2), consumed within experiment; numeric specific qNH4 not stated (time course only in Fig 1A). Secretion values are measured concentrations/peaks (NH2OH 83.5 uM at 1 h; NOx peak 30.2 ppm; N2O peak 42.1 ppm). NO 0.2 mmol/L/h derived from model-predicted max 1 umol/h per 5 ml (=0.005 L): 1e-3 mmol / 0.005 L / h = 0.2 mmol/L/h; this is a dFBA-simulated maximum, not a measured net flux. No gDW conversion available (OD only) so specific per-gDW rates not derivable.

growth_records.strain: N. europaea ATCC 19718 + N. winogradskyi Nb-255
growth_records.medium: Experimental batch mineral salts medium (ammonium, coculture)

up.method: initial 5 mM NH4+ (2.5 mM (NH4)2SO4), consumed more rapidly than in N. europaea single culture (Fig 3A); no numeric specific rate stated
NO2- produced by N. europaea then oxidized by N. winogradskyi; ~8 h to consume accumulated NO2- (Fig 3A)

sec.method: final nitrification product (Fig 3A time course); no numeric specific rate stated
measured headspace peak 53.7 +/- 2.2 ppm (greater than sum of single cultures, P<0.005)
measured headspace peak 47.8 +/- 4.1 ppm
measured 54.4 +/- 7.5 uM after 1 h then consumed by 4 h

growth_records.cite: Mellbye et al. Genome-Scale, Constraint-Based Modeling of Nitrogen Oxide Fluxes during Coculture of Nitrosomonas europaea and Nitrobacter winogradskyi. mSystems (2018). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00170-17. [PMC5864417]
growth_records.snippet: N. europaea, N. winogradskyi, and coculture experimental cultures were suspended to an optical density at 600 nm (OD600) of 0.2, 0.05, and 0.25, respectively... The coculture... produced approximately 53.7 +/- 2.2 and 47.8 +/- 4.1 ppm of NO
growth_records.notes: Coculture batch on N. europaea ammonium medium (verbatim recipe). Combined inoculum OD600 0.25, 30C, pH 7.8, 200 rpm, closed batch 10 h, aerobic (transient microaerobic from O2 competition). No mu reported for batch -> null. Substrate NH4+ 5 mM initial (2.5 mM (NH4)2SO4), NO2- an intermediate. Secretion peaks measured: NOx 53.7 ppm, N2O 47.8 ppm, NH2OH 54.4 uM at 1 h. All are measured concentrations/peaks, not specific per-gDW rates; no gDW conversion available. culture_mode = co-culture (two organisms in one batch vessel).

growth_records.medium: N. europaea mineral medium (280 mg-N/L ammonium, EPPS-buffered)

up.units: sOUR reported per-cell in figures only (not numeric in text)
up.method: sOUR = respirogram DO-slope / cell count; values figure-only
respirometry; numeric values only in Fig 2 A2-C2

sec.units: ~88-90% of NH3-N oxidised to NO2- (extent, not a rate)
sec.method: diazotization; 90+/-10% (no nitrite) at DO 1.5

yields.note: cell yield not impacted by DO; NH3->NO2- conversion 88-90%

growth_records.cite: Yu et al. Strategies of Nitrosomonas europaea 19718 to counter low dissolved oxygen and high nitrite concentrations. BMC Microbiology (2010). [PMC2844404]
growth_records.method: reported (max specific growth rate by non-linear estimation of cell-count growth curve)
growth_records.snippet: Estimates of the maximum specific growth rate ... at DO = 0.5 mg O2/L (0.043 +/- 0.005 h-1)... were not statistically different at alpha=0.05.
growth_records.notes: Batch chemolithoautotroph N. europaea ATCC 19718, DO=0.5 mg O2/L. Reported mu_max=0.043+/-0.005 h-1 (non-linear estimation). td=ln2/0.043=16.12 h. Substrate NH3 (280 mg-N/L); product NO2- (~88-90% of NH3-N oxidised). sOUR (specific O2 uptake, per cell) reported only graphically in Fig 2 (no numeric value in text/tables), so no mmol/gDW/h uptake rate can be curated. Temperature not stated in text.

growth_records.medium: N. europaea medium + 280 mg NO2--N/L added, DO 1.5 mg O2/L

up.method: gas-sensing electrode / diazotization

sec.method: diazotization
chemiluminescence

yields.note: overall cell yield not impacted by added nitrite; NH3->NO2- 88+/-5%

growth_records.cite: Yu et al. Strategies of Nitrosomonas europaea 19718 to counter low dissolved oxygen and high nitrite concentrations. BMC Microbiology (2010). [PMC2844404]
growth_records.method: growth occurred (extended lag) but mu not separately quantified in text
growth_records.snippet: An initial NO2- concentration of 280 mg NO2--N/L and DO = 1.5 mg O2/L ... the overall cell yield was not impacted. The extent of NH3 oxidized to NO2- in the presence of 280 mg NO2--N/L (88+/-5%, n=2).
growth_records.notes: N. europaea batch, DO 1.5 mg O2/L WITH 280 mg NO2--N/L added: growth occurred after extended lag; cell yield unaffected; NH3->NO2- 88+/-5%. mu not numerically reported for this condition. Companion condition: 560 mg NO2--N/L -> NO GROWTH after 2 weeks (complete inhibition; a no-growth negative datapoint).

growth_records.medium: SynM (modified autotrophic medium), fresh ACS-grade chemicals (Period 1)

up.units: mg N / mg biomass(O2-equiv) / day (derived qS)
up.method: calculated qS = D / fS = 0.2 day-1 / 0.06 = 3.33; caveat: fS is in mg biomass-as-oxygen-equivalents per mg N, so this is an oxygen-equivalent-basis specific uptake, not a true dry-weight mmol/gDW/h
Ammonia removal efficiency measured at 97.2 +/- 1.5% (Period 1); influent ammonium from (NH4)2SO4 2640 mg/L ~= 559 mg N/L; specific uptake numeric not tabulated in main text

up.units: specific OUR (sOUR_max) measured but numeric value not given in main text
up.method: YSI O2 electrode, DO logged every 10 s; sOUR = OUR/cell concentration

up.units: mol C per mol N processed
up.method: reported stoichiometric requirement; 320 mg C/day supplied as saturated NaHCO3 at 2 L scale

sec.units: primary product of ammonia oxidation (NH3 -> NO2-); >96% of influent ammonia converted; specific rate not tabulated
sec.method: Griess reagent nitrite assay

sec.units: mg N / day (whole 2 L reactor, not specific)
sec.method: nitrogen mass balance; total N loss 5.4 mg N/day = 2 mg N/day biomass + ~3.4 mg N/day gaseous N oxides

yields.Yxs_biomass_per_N: 0.06 +/- 0.01 mg biomass (as O2 equivalents) / mg N (Period 1)
yields.free_energy_efficiency: 4.4 +/- 1.6% (Period 1); energy from NH3 oxidation to nitrite = 230 kJ/mol N; energy in biomass = 305 kJ/mol biomass C

growth_records.cite: Wendell O. Khunjar et al. Biomass Production from Electricity Using Ammonia as an Electron Carrier in a Reverse Microbial Fuel Cell. PLoS ONE (2012). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0044846. [PMC3446996]
growth_records.snippet: Nitrosomonas europaea ATCC 19718 was cultured in a chemostat (2 L; dilution rate = 0.2 day-1) utilizing modified autotrophic medium (designated SynM)... Period 1 = growth on synthetic media prepared from ACS grade chemicals... Period 1 mu_m
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote: chemolithoautotrophic ammonia-oxidizing bacterium. Chemostat steady state => mu = D = 0.2 day-1 = 0.2/24 = 0.00833 h-1. Doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.00833 = 83.2 h (= ln2/0.2 = 3.47 day). Derived ammonia qS = mu/Yxs = D/fS = 0.2 day-1 / 0.06 (mg biomass-O2equiv per mg N) = 3.33 mg N per mg biomass per day; UNIT CAVEAT: fS reported as oxygen-equivalents biomass, so true dry-weight mmol/gDW/h not directly recoverable without COD->VSS conversion (~1.42 mgCOD/mgbiomass). mu_max (0.50 day-1) is a separate batch-extant kinetic parameter, NOT the chemostat operating growth rate. Temperature not stated in paper. pH 7.5+/-0.01 controlled with NaHCO3. NH3 removal 97.2+/-1.5%. All nutrients (ammonia, O2, inorganic carbon, trace metals) in excess.

growth_records.medium: SynM spent media, electrochemically regenerated once (Period 2)

up.units: mg N / mg biomass(O2-equiv) / day (derived qS)
up.method: calculated qS = D / fS = 0.2 day-1 / 0.05 = 4.0; same oxygen-equivalent-basis caveat as Period 1
gas-sensing electrode / mass balance

sec.units: primary product of ammonia oxidation; specific rate not tabulated
sec.method: Griess reagent

yields.Yxs_biomass_per_N: 0.05 (Table fS) / 0.06 +/- 0.02 (Results text) mg biomass (as O2 equivalents) / mg N (Period 2)

growth_records.cite: Wendell O. Khunjar et al. Biomass Production from Electricity Using Ammonia as an Electron Carrier in a Reverse Microbial Fuel Cell. PLoS ONE (2012). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0044846. [PMC3446996]
growth_records.snippet: Period 2 (growth on electrochemically generated ammonia from period 1 effluent) = 97.9+/-1.3%... Period 2 mu_max 0.36+/-0.07, K_NH3 2.57+/-0.63, fS 0.05+/-0.02, free energy efficiency 5.1+/-1.0%.
growth_records.notes: Same chemostat (D=0.2 day-1 => mu=0.00833 h-1, td=83.2 h). Medium = Period-1 spent media with nitrite electrochemically regenerated to ammonia. Derived ammonia qS = D/fS = 0.2/0.05 = 4.0 mg N per mg biomass(O2-equiv) per day (unit caveat as Period 1). mu_max 0.36 day-1 is batch-extant estimate, not chemostat operating rate. Biokinetic params statistically unchanged vs Period 1 (P=0.11 for mu_max). Temperature not stated.

growth_records.medium: SynM spent media, electrochemically regenerated twice (Period 3)

up.units: mg N / mg biomass(O2-equiv) / day (derived qS)
up.method: calculated qS = D / fS = 0.2 day-1 / 0.05 = 4.0; oxygen-equivalent-basis caveat as Period 1
gas-sensing electrode / mass balance

sec.units: primary product of ammonia oxidation; specific rate not tabulated
sec.method: Griess reagent

yields.Yxs_biomass_per_N: 0.05 (Table fS) / 0.06 +/- 0.01 (Results text) mg biomass (as O2 equivalents) / mg N (Period 3)

growth_records.cite: Wendell O. Khunjar et al. Biomass Production from Electricity Using Ammonia as an Electron Carrier in a Reverse Microbial Fuel Cell. PLoS ONE (2012). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0044846. [PMC3446996]
growth_records.snippet: Period 3 (growth on electrochemically generated ammonia from period 2 effluent) = 95.5+/-2.7%... Period 3 mu_max 0.24+/-0.08, K_NH3 3.19+/-1.34, fS 0.05+/-0.02, free energy efficiency 4.9+/-0.6%.
growth_records.notes: Same chemostat (D=0.2 day-1 => mu=0.00833 h-1, td=83.2 h). Medium = twice electrochemically regenerated ammonia. Derived ammonia qS = D/fS = 0.2/0.05 = 4.0 mg N per mg biomass(O2-equiv) per day (unit caveat as Period 1). mu_max 0.24 day-1 is batch-extant estimate; authors report biokinetic params statistically unchanged across Periods 1-3 (P=0.11 mu_max, P=0.15 K_NH3, P=0.48 fS). Overall biomass production power = 0.386 kWhr/g biomass. Temperature not stated.
